zh.lifeSummary: 當Gillecholum Ó Duibhne Campbell - First Lord of Lochowe於1006年之後出生在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾洛霍時，他的父親Duibhne O'Duibhne9054歲，母親Grain O Neill9009歲。他和Cambus Bellus Beauchamp Heiress of Beauchamp至少有6個兒子。他去世於1066年在法國豪-諾門迪，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿吉爾吉爾可雷南。

nameMeanings.name: Ó
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

variants.name: Oh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

nameMeanings.name: Campbell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Camble
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Campbell  .

variants.name: Campbel
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Campbell  .

variants.name: Cambell
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Campbell  .

variants.name: Campell
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: variant of   Campbell   (see also   Camel  ).

variants.name: Beauchamp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Camel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Napoleon, Patrice.

English (of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Norman French came(i)l ‘camel’ (from Latin camelus, classical Greek kamēlos), possibly denoting a clumsy person or someone with long legs. It may also be a topographic or habitational name referring to a house with a sign depicting a camel (although surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English); compare 5 below.
English: possibly a habitational name from Queen Camel and West Camel in Somerset, early recorded as Camel(le), possibly a Celtic name from canto- ‘border, district’ and mēl ‘bare hill’.

en.lifeSummary: When Gillecholum Ó Duibhne Campbell - First Lord of Lochowe was born after 1006, in Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Duibhne O'Duibhne, was 9054 and his mother, Grain O Neill, was 9009. He had at least 6 sons with Cambus Bellus Beauchamp Heiress of Beauchamp. He died in 1066, in Upper Normandy, France, and was buried in Kilchrenan, Argyll, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gillecholum Ó Duibhne Campbell - First Lord of Lochowe est né après 1006, à Lochawe, Argyll, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Duibhne O'Duibhne, avait 9054 ans et sa mère, Grain O Neill, avait 9009 ans. Il a eu au moins 6 fils avec Cambus Bellus Beauchamp Heiress of Beauchamp. Il est décédé en 1066, à Haute-Normandie, France, et a été enterré à Kilchrenan, Argyll, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gillecholum Ó Duibhne Campbell - First Lord of Lochowe nach 1006 in Lochawe, Argyll, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Duibhne O'Duibhne 9054 und seine Mutter Grain O Neill 9009. Er hatte mit Cambus Bellus Beauchamp Heiress of Beauchamp mindestens 6 Söhne. Er starb 1066 in Haute-Normandie, Frankreich und wurde in Kilchrenan, Argyll, Scotland begraben.

Lui avuto almeno 6 figli con Cambus Bellus Beauchamp Heiress of Beauchamp. Morì nel 1066, nel Haute-Normandie, Francia, e fu sepolto a Kilchrenan, Argyll, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Gillecholum Ó Duibhne Campbell - First Lord of Lochoweが1006年以後にイギリススコットランドアルグイルロチャウで生まれた時，父Duibhne O'Duibhneは9054歳，母Grain O Neillは9009歳であった。彼はCambus Bellus Beauchamp Heiress of Beauchampとの間に少なくとも6人の息子をもうけた。彼は1066年にフランスハウテ-ノーマンディで死去し，スコットランドアルグイルキルチレナンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Gillecholum Ó Duibhne Campbell - First Lord of Lochowe(이)가 1006년 이후 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 럭아베에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Duibhne O'Duibhne(은)는 9054세였고, 그의 어머니 Grain O Neill(은)는 9009세였다. 그는 Cambus Bellus Beauchamp Heiress of Beauchamp(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1066년 프랑스 하테-노먼디에서 사망했으며 스컷랜드 아그이 킬크리난에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gillecholum Ó Duibhne Campbell - First Lord of Lochowe родился после 1006 г. в местности лочав, аргилл, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Duibhne O'Duibhne было 9054 года, а матери, которую звали Grain O Neill, 9009. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей от Cambus Bellus Beauchamp Heiress of Beauchamp. Он умер в 1066 г. в местности хауте-норманди, Франция и был похоронен в местности килчренан, аргилл, Шотландия.
